---
title: "AI for UVM Economics"
subtitle: "Let's become AI masters"
---

## AI Bootcamp — Spring 2026

Two lunch sessions introducing AI tools for teaching and research. No prior experience required.

### Schedule

| Session | Date | Focus |
|---------|------|-------|
| [Session 1](session1.qmd) | **Wed, April 22, 11:00--12:30** Old. Mill A500 | Getting started: what AI tools exist, how they work, live demos |
| [Session 2](session2.qmd) | **Mon, April 27, 11:00--12:30** Old Mill A500 | Research pipelines, web scraping, automated grading |

### Before you arrive

No installation needed — both sessions are watch-along demos. See the **[Getting Set Up](prep.qmd)** guide if you want to try things on your own afterward.

If you want broader reading beyond the bootcamp itself, see **[Outside Resources](resources.qmd)**.

---

### Slide templates

Here's what the slide format looks like — four quick demos:

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="25%"}
[**Moon** (dark)](slides/demo-title.html){target="_blank"}
:::
::: {.column width="25%"}
[**Serif** (classic)](slides/demo-columns.html){target="_blank"}
:::
::: {.column width="25%"}
[**Solarized** (warm)](slides/demo-table.html){target="_blank"}
:::
::: {.column width="25%"}
[**Seminar** (plain)](slides/demo-seminar.html){target="_blank"}
:::
::::

---

Built with [Quarto](https://quarto.org). Source on [GitHub](https://github.com/eabeam/uvmecon-ai).
